title: 自主网络运营框架的新智能体
author: Naresh Rao, Prashant Kumar
content:
Last year, Google Cloud unveiled the Autonomous Network Operations framework, a comprehensive blueprint designed to help Communication Service Providers (CSPs) transition from manual management to zero-touch operations. As part of that launch, we introduced a RAN AI agent as a Proof-of-Value (PoV) asset, demonstrating how generative AI could autonomously optimize radio access networks (RAN).

Today, we’re announcing the evolution of our framework. Google Cloud. In partnership with Future Connections, we’re expanding the framework's capabilities to move beyond isolated tasks toward agentic AI that reasons and executes across the entire telecom stack. We’re introducing two critical innovations integrated directly into the umbrella: the Autonomous Data Steward and the Core Network VoLTE Agent, which New Zealand telecom provider One NZ has deployed into its operations.

"When we launched the Autonomous Network Operations Framework, our goal was to provide a blueprint for the future of telecom. Today, we are solidifying that framework by developing and launching critical new agents under the Autonomous Network umbrella. One NZ is deploying the Core Network VoLTE Agent, which is a testament to this progress, demonstrating how we are empowering operators to move beyond isolated use cases and truly operationalize AI at scale." - Muninder Sambi, VP & GM of Products, Google Distributed Cloud

The industry bottleneck: Why agents struggle to scale

While the industry has successfully deployed individual agents, scaling to a fully autonomous ecosystem has revealed a fundamental infrastructure challenge: data access is broken. Currently, every organization developing a new AI agent faces the same hurdles:

  • Data silos: Data is locked in rigid schemas and scattered across domains. For example, RAN and Core in BigQuery or Probes in Cloud Storage. 
  • Semantic complexity: Without semantic context, data scientists can spend months deciphering cryptic vendor-specific counters (e.g., "pmCounterNumber_487") rather than building models.
  • Access issues: Each new agent requires a custom integration project, making rapid scaling challenging.

To solve this, we must first activate the data infrastructure’s intelligence. This requires a dual-layered approach: an intelligent agent to govern the data foundation and specialized agents to act upon it.

Expanding the framework: The Autonomous Data Steward

The Autonomous Data Steward is a Gemini-powered agent that serves as the intelligent backbone of the framework. It doesn’t just store data; it dynamically provisions it on demand for other agents to use.

Built on Google Cloud, the Steward utilizes a "zero-copy" architecture. Using

Dataplex Universal Catalog
, it stores metadata pointers rather than duplicating massive datasets. This approach reduces storage costs by up to 70% while helping ensure agents can access real-time telemetry.

The Autonomous Data Steward further automates the DataOps lifecycle by leveraging Google’s advanced reasoning models to bridge the gap between human intent and technical execution. This enables:

  1. Semantic understanding: When a user or agent asks for data (e.g., "cell drop rate"), Gemini interprets the natural language intent and maps it to specific telecom counters using Dataplex Universal Catalog.

  2. Dynamic ETL creation: If the dataset doesn't exist, the Steward autonomously writes and executes Python code (via

    Dataproc
    ) to extract, clean, and join the data — reducing weeks of engineering to minutes.

  3. Cross-domain unification: The agent can generate federated queries that join RAN performance, Core session failures, and Probe errors into a single view, transparently uniting isolated domains.

The Core Network (VoLTE) Agent

We are moving from reactive monitoring to self-healing networks. OSS remediation agents don’t just alert humans to problems; they diagnose and solve them. By breaking down data silos, we are building a cognitive layer that turns months of engineering expertise into milliseconds of automated action.

The IMS Quality Agent (VoLTE Agent) is our specialized core network specialist. Built on the foundation of the Autonomous Data Steward, it provides excellent voice service in three ways:

  • Real-time monitoring: It continuously tracks critical metrics like Call Setup Success Rates, Mean Opinion Scores (MOS), and CSCF performance.
  • Intelligent root cause analysis: Powered by Gemini’s multimodal reasoning, the agent correlates complex signaling data (SIP/Diameter) with Core performance to instantly identify issues like CSCF overload.
  • Autonomous recommendations: The agent recommends corrective actions, such as adjusting call routing or rebalancing load.

When the VoLTE Agent detects a dropped call, it queries the data to instantly fetch correlated IMS signaling and probe captures — data that previously lived in disconnected systems.

One NZ is already deploying this agent to proactively manage voice quality, moving beyond manual maintenance toward a fully autonomous network.

"We're excited to partner with Future Connections and Google Cloud, using Gemini's advanced capabilities to deploy agentic workflows into our network. This is another meaningful progress step on our journey towards fully autonomous network operations." - Kieran Byrne, CTO, One NZ

Accelerating the journey to autonomous operations

These launches represent an exciting leap forward in the framework. By integrating the infrastructure intelligence of the Autonomous Data Steward agent with the domain-specific logic of the VoLTE Agent into the Autonomous Network Operations framework, we’re delivering:

  • For operations teams: A critical MTTR accelerator, slashing the time required to identify and resolve voice service issues. This helps teams move from detection to resolution in a fraction of the time, maintaining strong network performance.
  • For data teams: A deployment accelerator, collapsing the time between model development and live production. This allows data teams to bypass traditional bottlenecks of data operations and realize the value of new ML deployments almost instantly.

Together, we’ve open-sourced these core methodologies, allowing operators to build and customize their own agentic workflows.

Ready to get started? If you’re interested in gaining early access to the Autonomous Data Steward or the VoLTE Agent, or want to learn more about One NZ’s deployment of the agent, please reach out to your Google Account Team or Future Connections today.
aiSummary: Google Cloud 扩展了其自主网络运营框架，推出了两个由 Gemini 驱动的新智能体——自主数据管家和 VoLTE 智能体，旨在解决电信数据孤岛并实现网络自动修复。
oneSentenceSummary: Google Cloud 扩展了其自主网络运营框架，推出了两个由 Gemini 驱动的新智能体——自主数据管家和 VoLTE 智能体，旨在解决电信数据孤岛并实现网络自动修复。
summary: 本文详细介绍了 Google Cloud 通信服务提供商（CSP）框架的演进，从孤立的 AI 任务转向集成的智能体工作流。文章指出，数据孤岛和语义复杂性是扩展电信 AI 的主要瓶颈。为了解决这些问题，Google 推出了自主数据管家，利用 Gemini 自动化 DataOps，将自然语言映射到技术指标，并利用“零复制”架构降低成本。此外，由 One NZ 部署的核心网 VoLTE 智能体为语音服务提供实时监控和根因分析。这种综合方法旨在推动 CSP 向零接触、自愈式网络运营转型。

mainPoints.point: 数据孤岛和语义复杂性是电信行业扩展 AI 的主要瓶颈。
mainPoints.explanation: 电信数据通常被锁定在僵化的、特定供应商的架构中，迫使数据科学家花费数月时间破译晦涩的指标，而不是构建有影响力的模型。

mainPoints.point: 自主数据管家利用 Gemini 弥合自然语言意图与技术数据之间的鸿沟。
mainPoints.explanation: 该智能体能够解释诸如“小区掉话率”之类的自然语言查询，并将其映射到特定指标，同时自主生成 ETL 代码以统一跨域数据。

mainPoints.point: 通过 Dataplex 实现的“零复制”架构显著降低了存储成本并提高了实时访问能力。
mainPoints.explanation: 通过存储元数据指针而非复制海量数据集，该框架声称可降低高达 70% 的存储成本，同时确保智能体能够访问实时遥测数据。

mainPoints.point: VoLTE 智能体通过实时监控和多模态推理实现自愈网络。
mainPoints.explanation: 它将复杂的信令数据（SIP/Diameter）与核心网性能相关联，以识别根本原因并推荐纠正措施，例如负载再平衡。

mainPoints.point: 该框架加速了平均修复时间 (MTTR) 和整体 ML 部署生命周期。
mainPoints.explanation: 通过自动化数据发现和故障排除，运营团队能够更快地解决问题，数据团队则可以绕过开发与生产之间的传统瓶颈。

keyQuotes: 数据被锁定在僵化的架构中并分散在各个领域……数据科学家可能要花数月时间来破译特定供应商的晦涩指标。
自主数据管家是一个由 Gemini 驱动的智能体，它是该框架的智能骨干。
我们正在从反应式监控转向自愈网络。
通过打破数据孤岛，我们正在构建一个认知层，将数月的工程经验转化为毫秒级的自动化行动。
这是我们在迈向完全自主网络运营征程中取得的又一个具有意义的进展。
